name: Ometecuhtli
description: Ometecuhtli, whose name means 'Lord Two', is a primordial deity in Aztec mythology, representing the dualistic nature of existence. He resides with his consort, Omecihuatl ('Lady Two'), in Omeyocan, the highest level of heaven, a place of duality and origins. Together, they embody the masculine and feminine principles, creation and destruction, order and chaos, and are considered the origin of all gods and humanity. Ometecuhtli is a remote, almost inaccessible deity, rarely directly involved in the affairs of the world but fundamentally responsible for its existence. He represents the ultimate creative force, the source from which everything emanates, and the balance that sustains the cosmos. While not typically worshipped with temples or sacrifices like other Aztec gods, his presence is acknowledged as the underlying power behind all deities and natural phenomena. His influence is felt in the inherent duality of the Aztec worldview, where everything possesses both a positive and negative aspect. He is the foundation of the universe and the source of life itself, existing beyond human comprehension and intervention. He is also sometimes associated with the number two, symbolizing the inherent duality in all things. Ometecuhtli's power lies not in direct action but in the potential for creation and destruction that resides within the universe itself. He is the embodiment of that potential, a constant reminder of the delicate balance that must be maintained.
subtitle: Lord of Duality and Origin

keyMyths.title: Creation of the Gods
keyMyths.description: Ometecuhtli and Omecihuatl, residing in Omeyocan, birthed the four Tezcatlipocas, who in turn created the world and the other gods. This established their position as the primordial deities.

keyMyths.title: Residence in Omeyocan
keyMyths.description: Ometecuhtli and Omecihuatl dwell in the highest heaven, Omeyocan, a place of duality and the source of all existence. This highlights their remote and powerful nature.

keyMyths.title: The Dualistic Nature
keyMyths.description: Ometecuhtli embodies the concept of duality, representing both male and female, creation and destruction, order and chaos. This is a fundamental aspect of Aztec cosmology.
